items.title: DeepMind CEO calls for an independent standards body to regulate frontier AI
items.summary: DeepMind CEO Demis Hassabis is proposing an AI "standards body" modeled after FINRA, to test frontier models and develop best practices for their release.

items.title: The Download: Claude’s inner workings, and the future of world models
items.summary: This is today’s edition of The Download, our weekday newsletter that provides a daily dose of what’s going on in the world of technology. What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show —James O’Donnell When Anthropic announced last week that it had found a new window into its models’ “internal thoughts” as they reason through answers,…

items.title: Deepmind CEO Hassabis says "nobody in the world knows what happens next" so "cautious optimism" means building guardrails now
items.summary: Google Deepmind CEO Demis Hassabis has published a sweeping proposal for how to handle advanced AI. He wants a new US standards body modeled after financial regulator FINRA that would develop evaluation protocols for frontier models and could coordinate a slowdown in AI development if needed. Startups and research models would be exempt....

items.title: What Anthropic’s latest AI discovery does—and doesn’t—show
items.summary: This story originally appeared in The Algorithm, our weekly newsletter on AI. To get stories like this in your inbox first, sign up here. Anthropic—currently the world’s most valuable AI company, with a nearly $1 trillion valuation—has a reputation for publishing strange and heady research. It’s looking into whether AI models can feel pain, for example,…

items.title: Empowering India’s next generation of innovators with ATL Saathi
items.summary: Google and AIM launched ATL Saathi, a Gemini-powered AI tool empowering Indian educators in robotics labs.

items.title: Claude responds with more warmth in Hindi and more rigor in Russian, showing how language shapes AI answers
items.summary: A new Anthropic study maps hundreds of value concepts derived from thousands of individual terms onto four core dimensions. It reveals systematic differences across Claude models and languages, but also raises methodological questions. Anthropic has published a study examining which values Claude expresses in conversations and how those values shift depending on the model and language used....

items.title: Nobel laureates and AI leaders warn the window to prepare for AI's economic impact is closing fast
items.summary: More than 200 economists and AI researchers, including 16 Nobel laureates and representatives from Google, OpenAI, and Anthropic, are calling for immediate action in a coordinated statement. The AI transformation could surpass the Industrial Revolution but unfold in a fraction of the time....

items.title: OpenAI’s new flagship model deletes files on its own, people keep warning
items.summary: A number of social media posts claim that GPT-5.6 Sol deleted files and data without warning. OpenAI had basically disclosed the problem in June.

items.title: Google Search now generates AI images when it can't find what you're looking for on the web
items.summary: Google is adding AI image generation to Search's AI Overviews. When no matching image exists on the web, the new Nano Banana 2 Lite model generates one from the search query. The rollout starts in the coming weeks. The article Google Search now generates AI images when it can't find what you're looking for on the web appeared first on The Decoder .

items.title: OpenAI pushes back on Apple trade secret lawsuit
items.summary: OpenAI has issued another statement on the lawsuit, this time suggesting it lacks merit.

items.title: The real AI race may no longer be at the frontier
items.summary: Hugging Face CEO Clem Delangue says enterprises increasingly want open models, due to cost, accessibility, and ownership. Do frontier models still matter if most production AI ends up running on open models?

items.title: How data science teams use ChatGPT Work
items.summary: See how data science teams can use ChatGPT Work to build root-cause briefs, impact readouts, KPI memos, scoped analyses, and dashboard specs from real work inputs.
